About me

A little bit
about the builder.

I'm a technology entrepreneur based in Seattle, with an MBA (GPA 3.8) from Northwest University and a career that spans fintech business development, AI infrastructure, and blockchain platform development. Put simply: I build things, I break things, and I sell things — often all three at once.

On the business side, I grow partner ecosystems and close deals — I managed partnerships across 400+ global organizations at SWFI, sovereign wealth funds and ADIA among them, generating $250K+ in revenue. On the technical side, I operate a multi-GPU AI compute cluster, build full-stack platforms, and conduct smart-contract security research across Solana/Anchor and EVM.

I'm also a published author of sixteen books — from technical references on engineering and finance to thrillers, sci-fi, and fantasy — an independent trader across traditional and on-chain markets, and a writer and researcher for automotive media. Wherever I work, I like to own the outcome end to end.

Off the clock, I'm a lifelong gamer — I've created and modded custom content for Discord communities, from sticker packs to animated GIFs. 🎮

Business Development Ethereum L1 / Solidity Smart-Contract Security AI / GPU Infra Trading Author Gaming

Author sixteen published books spanning technical non-fiction and fiction — finance, engineering, and science through thrillers, sci-fi, and fantasy; write and research for automotive media; and maintain an active trading practice across traditional and on-chain markets.

Partnerships

SWFI — Partner ecosystem & BD

Partner-ecosystem business development at platform scale — grew SWFI's network to 400+ member organizations by opening first-contact relationships, onboarding partners, and warming and qualifying each one before handoff, generating $250K+ in revenue. Ran the marketing and outreach and built the data and analytics behind SWFI's institutional platform. Convened sovereign wealth funds and institutional partners worldwide (ADIA, SkyBridge Capital & many more) for a major international conference — and earned a handwritten letter from the Mayor of Budapest for the tourism boost from bringing global partners together.

PartnershipsDemand Gen$250K+
SWFI Summit ↗
Web3 · Business

Koalaified — professional networking, reimagined

Founded and built Koalaified.com — a ground-up rethink of how people network and find work online. The idea was to fold the best of a social platform, a professional network, and a job board into one product, then rebuild it around trust: real, verified job listings instead of ghost postings, genuine connections instead of vanity metrics, and AI-assisted networking and career discovery that actually helps people get hired. I owned it end to end — product, platform, front-end, and the partner program behind it — with transparency as the default, not a feature.

Security research

Breaking smart
contracts.

Smart-contract security on Solana/Anchor & EVM — manual source review, stateful invariant fuzzing, and reproducible proofs-of-concept.

6verified findings
4runnable PoCs
2production protocols reviewed

Verified findings

Manual review of open-source Solana / Anchor programs. Each critical ships a faithful, runnable PoC that mirrors the on-chain u128 arithmetic. PoCs on GitHub ↗

TargetVulnerabilitySeverityProof
FrankSol · stake_v2sol_to_franksol divides before multiplying → staking DoS + mispricingCriticalpoc_sol_to_franksol.mjs
FrankSol · stake_v2withdraw_from_yield applies yield PnL with inverted sign → insolvencyCriticalpoc_pnl_inversion.mjs
stake-flowunstake_locked ignores reward_debt → reward double-claim → vault drainCriticalpoc_stakeflow_double_claim.mjs
Zenonescape_amount validated against wrong (unset) field → curve funds lockedHighpoc_zenon_escape_amount.mjs
Zenoninitialize_market has no authority check → anyone creates marketsMediumsource review
MissionXUnconstrained init owner (literal // TODO) → protocol takeoverCriticalsource review

Reviewed clean

Real audit work is mostly disproving attack hypotheses. Two production, audited protocols reviewed in depth — no exploitable findings, the expected and demonstrable outcome for hardened code.

Morpho Midnight

0 violations
Solidity · fixed-rate lending · $400k Cantina competition · formally verified

Built a stateful invariant fuzzer in Foundry — two solvency invariants plus a reentrancy attacker across 128,000 randomized operation sequences (takes, liquidations, bad-debt realization, fee claims, callback reentrancy). Result: 0 invariant violations. Harness on GitHub ↗

MarginFi v2

No finding
Rust / Anchor · live lending protocol · $500k bug bounty

Reviewed the cross-protocol integration surface (Kamino / Drift / JupLend / Solend): oracle exchange-rate valuation (price and confidence scaled consistently) and withdraw paths with expected-vs-actual CPI reconciliation. Result: no exploitable finding.

Tooling stood up

  • Foundry (forge/anvil) — EVM build + invariant fuzzing, incl. bleeding-edge evm_version = osaka
  • Solana SBF toolchain — Rust + Agave cargo build-sbf, compiled a real Anchor program to BPF
  • Node / BigInt PoCs — faithful u128 arithmetic reproductions for root-cause proofs

Honest scope

The six findings come from the Solana Audit Arena — a practice arena of intentionally-vulnerable, AI-generated programs. They demonstrate real skill and method, not paid or novel disclosures.

Morpho and MarginFi are real, audited protocols; the clean results reflect genuine review, not lack of effort. Stating scope plainly is the point — it's what a serious audit team wants to see.

Next: building a public track record via CodeHawks First Flights → Code4rena / Sherlock contests.

All the code on GitHub ↗

Dec 2018 — 2021

Finance Research Analyst & BD Coordinator

SWFI (Sovereign Wealth Fund Institute)

Managed business development, data, and financial transactions across 400+ institutional organizations; led demand generation generating $250K+ in revenue. Opened first-contact communication lines with new partners and prospects, warming and qualifying each relationship before handing it to the sales team. Brought sovereign wealth funds, institutional investors, and funds from around the world together for a major international financial conference — engaging the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority (ADIA), SkyBridge Capital, and many others — through global outreach and email marketing. Ran email and content marketing, and built and maintained the AUM (assets-under-management) datasets and analytics that powered SWFI's Bloomberg-style institutional data terminal — spanning university endowments and funds across global markets — presenting insights directly to the President and VP. Received a handwritten letter from the Mayor of Budapest recognizing the tourism boost driven by convening global partners in the city.

2014 — 2017

Founder & Operator

NeonNightz

Founded and operated a custom apparel and merchandise printing business — screen-printed apparel, custom stickers, and branded merchandise for businesses, brands, and events. Owned the full cycle end to end: client relationships, design, production, fulfillment, and day-to-day operations.

2014 — 2017

Manufacturing & Production Operations

Woodinville, WA

Concurrently worked in manufacturing and line operations at a Woodinville-area manufacturer, supporting production runs and floor operations with a focus on throughput, quality, and efficiency.

2012

Automotive Service & Lot Operations

Aston Martin

Worked service and lot operations for a luxury performance marque — vehicle handling, prep, and service-floor logistics. Early hands-on time around high-performance machinery that still informs how I write about cars today.
